_meta.supplemented: Mass, gravity, and average surface temperatures not listed on NASA facts pages were filled from NASA Planetary Fact Sheet (nssdc.gsfc.nasa.gov/planetary/factsheet) — standard published values.

sun.name: Sun
sun.funFact: The Sun contains 99.86% of the mass of the entire solar system. Light from its surface takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.

planets.name: Mercury
planets.atmosphere: Exosphere — oxygen, sodium, hydrogen, helium, potassium
planets.funFact: At certain spots on Mercury, the Sun appears to rise briefly, set, and rise again — a quirk caused by its eccentric orbit and slow rotation.

planets.name: Venus
planets.atmosphere: 96% carbon dioxide, 3.5% nitrogen, clouds of sulfuric acid
planets.funFact: Venus rotates backward, and its day is longer than its year. If you stood on its surface, the Sun would rise in the west and set in the east.

planets.name: Earth
planets.funFact: If the Sun were as tall as a typical front door, Earth would be the size of a nickel — and the only world we know where life flourishes.

planets.name: Mars
planets.funFact: Valles Marineris is long enough to stretch from California to New York — about 4,000 km — making it the largest canyon in the solar system.

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.funFact: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm larger than Earth that has been raging for hundreds of years — possibly since before telescopes first saw it.

planets.name: Saturn
planets.funFact: Saturn is the only planet in our solar system with an average density less than water — theoretically, it could float in a bathtub.

planets.name: Uranus
planets.atmosphere: 83% hydrogen, 15% helium, 2% methane (gives it the blue tint)
planets.funFact: Uranus rotates on its side at a 97.77° tilt, rolling around the Sun like a ball — and it endures the most extreme seasons in the solar system.

planets.name: Neptune
planets.funFact: Neptune's winds can exceed 1,200 mph — making it the solar system's windiest world, despite receiving only a fraction of Earth's sunlight.
